prerequisites: An Azure subscription with an Azure Storage account. The storage account name supplies the azure_blob_storage_account_name config field.
A blob container in the storage account to receive the data. Its name supplies the required azure_blob_storage_container_name config field.

Credentials for one supported authorization method: a storage account access key, a Microsoft Entra ID service principal (client ID, client secret, tenant ID), or a shared access signature (SAS) token.
A chosen output file format for the written objects, supplied via the required format config field.

Optionally, a custom storage endpoint suffix via azure_blob_storage_endpoint_domain_name if you are not using the default public Azure endpoint (blob.core.windows.net), for example in a sovereign or private cloud.

authMethods.name: Storage account access key (Shared Key)
authMethods.summary: Authorize with one of the storage account's two access keys. Provide the key in the azure_blob_storage_account_key field together with the azure_blob_storage_account_name. Account keys grant full access to the storage account and can be viewed or rotated in the Azure portal under the account's Access keys page.

authMethods.name: Microsoft Entra ID service principal (OAuth client credentials)
authMethods.summary: Authorize with a Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) application/service principal. Provide azure_client_id, azure_client_secret, and azure_tenant_id. The principal must be assigned an Azure RBAC role that grants blob data access (for example Storage Blob Data Contributor) on the storage account or container.

authMethods.name: Shared access signature (SAS) token
authMethods.summary: Authorize with a shared access signature supplied in the shared_access_signature field. A SAS delegates limited, time-bound access to the storage account or container with the permissions encoded in the token, without exposing an account key.

setupSteps.title: Create or identify the storage account and container
setupSteps.body: In the Azure portal, create or select an Azure Storage account, then create the blob container that will receive the data. Record the storage account name for azure_blob_storage_account_name and the container name for azure_blob_storage_container_name.

setupSteps.title: Choose and obtain credentials for one authorization method
setupSteps.body: Pick one: (a) copy an account access key from the storage account's Access keys page for azure_blob_storage_account_key; (b) register a Microsoft Entra ID application, create a client secret, and supply azure_client_id, azure_client_secret, and azure_tenant_id, granting the principal a blob data RBAC role such as Storage Blob Data Contributor; or (c) generate a shared access signature and supply it as shared_access_signature.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connection fields
setupSteps.body: Enter azure_blob_storage_account_name and azure_blob_storage_container_name. Set the required format field to your desired output file format. Provide the secret(s) for your chosen auth method. Leave azure_blob_storage_endpoint_domain_name empty to use the default public endpoint, or set it for a non-default cloud or custom domain.

setupSteps.title: Set optional tuning and verify access
setupSteps.body: Optionally set azure_blob_storage_spill_size to control how large staged files grow before being flushed to blob storage. Verify that the supplied credentials can write to the target container before running a sync.

sources.title: Authorize access to data in Azure Storage
Authorize access to blobs using Microsoft Entra ID
Grant limited access to data with shared access signatures (SAS)
